metaDescription: How to build a Zap that automatically pushes data like invoices and customers from other apps into QuickBooks Online without manual entry.
intro: When QuickBooks doesn't have a direct integration with a tool you use, Zapier can bridge the gap. By setting up a trigger-and-action workflow, you can automatically create invoices or customer records in QuickBooks whenever something happens elsewhere — a new lead, a completed payment, a new spreadsheet row. Here's how to set one up.

steps.heading: Create a new Zap
steps.body: Log into Zapier and click Create Zap to start a new automated workflow.

steps.heading: Choose your trigger app
steps.body: Select the source app, such as Google Sheets or Stripe, and pick a specific trigger event like New Row or New Successful Payment.

steps.heading: Connect and test the trigger
steps.body: Authenticate your source app account and fetch a test record to confirm the trigger data looks right.

steps.heading: Set QuickBooks as the action
steps.body: Search for QuickBooks Online in the action step, then choose the event you want, such as Create Invoice or Create Customer.

steps.heading: Map fields and go live
steps.body: Authenticate your QuickBooks account, map the trigger's fields into the matching QuickBooks inputs, test the step, then flip the Zap on.

faq.question: Do I need coding skills to set this up?
faq.answer: No, Zapier's trigger-and-action interface is built for no-code use — you're mostly picking options from dropdowns and mapping fields.

faq.question: How do I know the integration is working correctly?
faq.answer: Use the Test Step feature to check the JSON response before going live, then verify a test record actually appears in QuickBooks.

faq.question: Can I map more than basic fields like name and amount?
faq.answer: Yes, you can map details like product descriptions and currency values to specific QuickBooks ledger fields during setup.

faq.question: What happens if I turn the Zap off later?
faq.answer: Turning it off simply stops new automated runs — existing data already synced to QuickBooks stays untouched.
